old_value: {"id":"compress-claude-bn9","title":"CLI examples for all compression formats","description":"Create CLI examples demonstrating all three layers (L1 pure, L2 async, L3 sync) for each compression format: flate, gzip, zlib, lzw, bzip2, and checksum. Examples live in lib/examples/ with moon.pkg configs.","acceptance_criteria":"All examples compile and run. L1/L3 examples work on wasm-gc, L2 async examples work on native target with #cfg guards.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:53:11Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:53:11Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-2tv","title":"Optimize CRC-32 with slicing-by-8","description":"CRC-32 is 427x slower than Go because Go uses hardware SSE4.2/CLMUL instructions. Since MoonBit/WASM can't use hardware CRC, implement slicing-by-8 (process 8 bytes per iteration with 8 precomputed tables) to get ~8x improvement over current byte-at-a-time approach.

**Current**: 110.9 µs/10KB (byte-at-a-time table lookup)
**Go**: 0.26 µs/10KB (hardware CRC)
**Target**: ~14 µs/10KB (slicing-by-8, still ~54x slower than hardware but 8x better)","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Implement 8-table slicing-by-8 CRC-32
- [ ] CRC-32 benchmark improves by at least 5x
- [ ] All checksum tests still pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:32:50Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:32:50Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-2tv","title":"Optimize CRC-32 with slicing-by-8","description":"CRC-32 is 427x slower than Go because Go uses hardware SSE4.2/CLMUL instructions. Since MoonBit/WASM can't use hardware CRC, implement slicing-by-8 (process 8 bytes per iteration with 8 precomputed tables) to get ~8x improvement over current byte-at-a-time approach.

**Current**: 110.9 µs/10KB (byte-at-a-time table lookup)
**Go**: 0.26 µs/10KB (hardware CRC)
**Target**: ~14 µs/10KB (slicing-by-8, still ~54x slower than hardware but 8x better)","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Implement 8-table slicing-by-8 CRC-32
- [ ] CRC-32 benchmark improves by at least 5x
- [ ] All checksum tests still pass","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:32:50Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:59:16Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-2jw","title":"Optimize Adler-32 with SWAR/block processing","description":"Adler-32 is 45x slower than Go. Implement block processing (NMAX=5552 chunk optimization) and potentially SWAR tricks to reduce overhead.

**Current**: 97.4 µs/10KB
**Go**: 2.15 µs/10KB","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Adler-32 benchmark improves by at least 3x
- [ ] All checksum tests still pass","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:00Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:00Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-2jw","title":"Optimize Adler-32 with SWAR/block processing","description":"Adler-32 is 45x slower than Go. Implement block processing (NMAX=5552 chunk optimization) and potentially SWAR tricks to reduce overhead.

**Current**: 97.4 µs/10KB
**Go**: 2.15 µs/10KB","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Adler-32 benchmark improves by at least 3x

{"id":"compress-claude-taf","title":"Optimize Huffman decoder with lookup tables","description":"The current Huffman decoder in flate/decompress decodes one bit at a time. Go uses a two-level table lookup (chunks + links) that decodes multiple bits per lookup. This is the #1 bottleneck — decompression is 49x slower than Go.

**Current**: bit-by-bit tree traversal in `HuffmanDecoder::decode`
**Target**: single-lookup for codes ≤9 bits, two-level for longer codes
**Impact**: Should bring decompression from ~49x to ~5-10x slower than Go","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Huffman decoder uses precomputed lookup table (≥9-bit first level)
- [ ] flate decompress benchmark improves by at least 5x
- [ ] All existing tests still pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:32:43Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:32:43Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-taf","title":"Optimize Huffman decoder with lookup tables","description":"The current Huffman decoder in flate/decompress decodes one bit at a time. Go uses a two-level table lookup (chunks + links) that decodes multiple bits per lookup. This is the #1 bottleneck — decompression is 49x slower than Go.

**Current**: bit-by-bit tree traversal in `HuffmanDecoder::decode`
**Target**: single-lookup for codes ≤9 bits, two-level for longer codes
**Impact**: Should bring decompression from ~49x to ~5-10x slower than Go","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Huffman decoder uses precomputed lookup table (≥9-bit first level)

{"id":"compress-claude-rlq","title":"Optimize DEFLATE compressor","description":"DEFLATE compression is 2-6x slower than Go depending on data type. Key areas:
- Hash chain lookup optimization
- Token encoding efficiency
- Lazy matching for levels 4-9
- Random data is 6x slower (worst case)","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] flate compress benchmarks improve by at least 2x
- [ ] All flate tests still pass","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:43Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:43Z"}

{"id":"compress-claude-y5b","title":"Expand benchmarks to larger data sizes and full corpus","description":"Per PLAN.md Section 9b, current benchmarks only cover 10KB data. Expand to:

- 100KB text workload
- 1MB text, zeros, random workloads
- JSON payload
- Binary payload
- bzip2 decompression benchmarks (currently missing)
- Per-level benchmarks for all compression levels

Also add tools/go_bench_test.go for automated Go comparison.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Benchmarks cover the full corpus from PLAN.md Section 9b
- [ ] bzip2 decompression benchmarks added
- [ ] Go benchmark harness in tools/ directory","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:44Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-07T02:33:44Z"}
